items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Irma Palasics died in 1999 when her home was robbed and she and her husband Gregor were beaten, tied up and gagged. Years of investigations followed, until Steve Fabriczy and Joseph Vekony were arrested after they were matched to two DNA samples in the couple's fridge.

gallery.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.

thumbnail.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.

items.title: 'Into the hands of Trump': Dan Duggan's bid to avoid extradition fails

children.content: Former United States Marine Corps pilot Dan Duggan’s Federal Court bid to avoid extradition from Australia is dismissed.

image.altText: Former US military pilot Daniel Edmund Duggan seen as a pilot in Tasmania

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: 'Your courage does not end': Prince Harry mingles with veterans in solo Canberra trip

children.content: The Duke of Sussex was in the nation's capital without his wife Meghan and addressed an Invictus Australia event.

items.title: A lucky chat, then a lifeline for men with prostate cancer

children.content: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

image.altText: A farmer in his 60s in a blue shirt stands in a paddock looking into the camera.

items.title: Video shows father and son illegally scuttling boat off NSW South Coast

children.content: The two fishermen were fined $15,000 for towing their vessel out to sea and illegally scuttling it.

image.altText: Trawler sinking at sea as fishermen watch on from another vessel.

items.title: Pressure increases on CIT CEO to stand down

children.content: An independent MLA is calling for the head of CIT to stand down, claiming statutory declarations show she "clearly misled" the board prior to her appointment, despite knowing about misconduct findings.

image.altText: Man with a beard wearing a black jacket.

items.title: 'Scary': Farmers and fishers face uncertain future as costs soar

children.content: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers selling in Canberra are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

items.title: The cause of the Viva Energy oil refinery fire under investigation. Here's what we know

children.content: Firefighters have managed to extinguish a blaze at one of Australia's major oil refineries. Here's what we know so far about the cause of the fire — and the possible impact on fuel prices.

image.altText: fire and smoke pictured above the Geelong refinery in the evening

items.title: Hanson wishes Coalition good luck, government focuses on fuel

children.content: This week in Canberra, Pauline Hanson learned imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, the defence budget was on the agenda and a fire threw the fuel crisis into sharp relief.

items.title: NSW premier told to resign after protest laws struck down by top court

children.content: The laws, rushed through parliament after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, have been found to be unconstitutional by the state's highest court.

items.title: Report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity rivals that of Ningaloo Reef

children.content: New research has revealed Darwin Harbour's biodiversity, including its wide range of rare species, rivals that of the World Heritage-protected Ningaloo Reef.

items.title: New data reveals age group spending up big

children.content: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

image.altText: A close-up of $100 notes fanning out from a wallet.

items.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel

children.content: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

items.title: Smoky Sydney as 5,000 hectares burned in hazard reduction blitz

children.content: Smoky conditions across Greater Sydney are tipped to ease tomorrow as the wind picks up, after firefighters had a productive week burning hazards around Sydney's fringes.

image.altText: a smoke column rises in a green forest seen from far away

items.title: Queensland massage therapist faces 18 sexual assault charges

children.content: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

image.altText: Photos of a hydronic cannabis operation supplied by police.

items.title: 'Snacks' ordered for alleged hit men's escape after underworld killing, court hears

children.content: Victoria's Supreme Court is told underworld figure Gavin Preston was cautious about his surroundings and "never" sat outside a cafe he regularly attended — other than the day he was shot dead.

image.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

items.title: Unemployment rate remains at 4.3pc in 'calm before the storm'

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Pandemic, 'ice epidemic' worsened Qld child safety pressures, ex-minister says

children.content: An inquiry into Queensland's child safety system hears the former minister was made aware of the sector's resource issues, as well as the "significant increase" in children entering residential care.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay workers told to take leave without pay or be sacked

children.content: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

items.title: 2032 Olympic rowing 'will be in Rockhampton': Mander

children.content: The Queensland sports minister has once again emphasised that the Brisbane 2032 Games will be an Olympics for the whole state, saying there are no alternatives planned for the contentious proposed rowing venue in Rockhampton.
